description: Curated nutrigenomics SNP panel for Yeliztli (P3-08). Covers 7 nutrient pathways with evidence-gated categorical scoring.

pathways.name: Folate Metabolism
pathways.description: Variants affecting folate/homocysteine metabolism via the one-carbon pathway.

GG.effect_summary: Normal MTHFR enzyme activity. Standard folate metabolism.

GA.effect_summary: Reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~65% of normal). Mildly elevated homocysteine possible.

AG.effect_summary: Reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~65% of normal). Mildly elevated homocysteine possible.

AA.effect_summary: Significantly reduced MTHFR enzyme activity (~30% of normal). Consider dietary folate sources and discuss methylfolate supplementation with a clinician.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider dietary folate sources (leafy greens, legumes) and discuss methylfolate supplementation with a clinician.

AA.effect_summary: Normal MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus.

AC.effect_summary: Mildly reduced MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus.

CA.effect_summary: Mildly reduced MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus.

CC.effect_summary: Moderately reduced MTHFR enzyme activity at A1298C locus. Effect is less pronounced than C677T.

snps.recommendation_text: Effect is milder than C677T. Compound heterozygosity (C677T + A1298C) may have additive effects on homocysteine levels.

AG.effect_summary: Mildly reduced methionine synthase reductase activity. May contribute to elevated homocysteine in combination with MTHFR variants.

GA.effect_summary: Mildly reduced methionine synthase reductase activity. May contribute to elevated homocysteine in combination with MTHFR variants.

snps.recommendation_text: Supportive role in folate metabolism. Effect is primarily relevant in combination with MTHFR variants.

pathways.name: Vitamin D
pathways.description: Variants affecting vitamin D synthesis, transport, and metabolism.

TT.effect_summary: Normal vitamin D binding protein levels.

TG.effect_summary: Modestly lower vitamin D binding protein levels. May have mildly reduced circulating 25(OH)D.

GT.effect_summary: Modestly lower vitamin D binding protein levels. May have mildly reduced circulating 25(OH)D.

GG.effect_summary: Significantly lower vitamin D binding protein levels. Associated with reduced circulating 25(OH)D.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider vitamin D status monitoring and adequate sun exposure or supplementation as directed by a clinician.

AA.effect_summary: Asp432/Asp432 (c.1296T/T; Gc1F isoform). Reduced vitamin D binding affinity, associated with lower circulating 25(OH)D.

AC.effect_summary: One Asp432 (c.1296T) allele; modestly reduced binding affinity and lower circulating 25(OH)D.

CA.effect_summary: One Asp432 (c.1296T) allele; modestly reduced binding affinity and lower circulating 25(OH)D.

CC.effect_summary: Glu432/Glu432 (c.1296G/G; Gc1S isoform). Higher/normal vitamin D binding protein affinity and circulating 25(OH)D (common European allele).

GA.effect_summary: One copy of the rs12794714 minor (A) allele. CYP2R1 vitamin D-pathway variant; its individual effect on 25(OH)D is low-confidence and not firmly established.

AG.effect_summary: One copy of the rs12794714 minor (A) allele. CYP2R1 vitamin D-pathway variant; its individual effect on 25(OH)D is low-confidence and not firmly established.

AA.effect_summary: Two copies of the rs12794714 minor (A) allele. Associated with altered 25(OH)D in some cohorts, but the direction of effect is unsettled and not firmly established.

snps.recommendation_text: rs12794714 is a CYP2R1 vitamin D-pathway variant whose individual effect on 25(OH)D is low-confidence and directionally unsettled across populations. Treated as supportive context only; interpret with a clinician alongside a measured 25(OH)D level.

GG.effect_summary: Reduced CYP2R1 expression. May contribute to lower vitamin D levels.

snps.recommendation_text: Supportive variant for vitamin D pathway assessment.

pathways.name: Vitamin B12
pathways.description: Variants affecting vitamin B12 absorption and transport.

GG.effect_summary: FUT2 rs602662 GG genotype. Associated with lower circulating vitamin B12, particularly with low animal-source intake.

GA.effect_summary: Heterozygous rs602662 genotype. Current panel evidence reserves the B12 concern for the replicated GG lower-B12 pattern.

AG.effect_summary: Heterozygous rs602662 genotype. Current panel evidence reserves the B12 concern for the replicated GG lower-B12 pattern.

AA.effect_summary: No rs602662 G allele. Not the lower-B12 genotype pattern in this panel.

snps.recommendation_text: People with the rs602662 GG genotype may benefit from monitoring B12 levels, particularly on plant-based diets.

pathways.name: Vitamin B6
pathways.description: Variants affecting vitamin B6 (pyridoxal 5'-phosphate) catabolism and circulating levels.

CT.effect_summary: Modestly lower circulating vitamin B6 levels.

TC.effect_summary: Modestly lower circulating vitamin B6 levels.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider dietary sources of vitamin B6 (poultry, fish, potatoes, chickpeas).

pathways.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
pathways.description: Variants affecting omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acid desaturation and metabolism.

TT.effect_summary: Typical FADS1 desaturase activity relative to rs174547 C carriers.

TC.effect_summary: C-carrier pattern associated with lower estimated FADS1 desaturase activity. Direct EPA/DHA intake may be more important than relying on endogenous conversion.

CT.effect_summary: C-carrier pattern associated with lower estimated FADS1 desaturase activity. Direct EPA/DHA intake may be more important than relying on endogenous conversion.

CC.effect_summary: Reduced FADS1 desaturase activity. C carriers have lower estimated D5D/D6D activity; direct EPA/DHA intake may be more important.

snps.recommendation_text: Consider direct dietary sources of EPA/DHA (fatty fish, algae-based supplements) if desaturase activity is reduced.

AG.effect_summary: Modestly altered omega-6 fatty acid levels.

GA.effect_summary: Modestly altered omega-6 fatty acid levels.

GG.effect_summary: Altered omega-6 fatty acid levels. Associated with higher inflammatory omega-6 to omega-3 ratio.

snps.recommendation_text: Balance omega-6 and omega-3 intake. Consider increasing omega-3-rich foods.

pathways.name: Iron Metabolism
pathways.description: Variants affecting iron absorption and storage, particularly hereditary hemochromatosis risk.

GG.effect_summary: No HFE C282Y mutation. Normal iron regulation at this locus.

GA.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE C282Y. Mildly increased iron absorption. Hemochromatosis risk depends on second HFE allele.

AG.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE C282Y. Mildly increased iron absorption. Hemochromatosis risk depends on second HFE allele.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous HFE C282Y. Significantly increased iron absorption. Discuss iron status monitoring with a clinician.

snps.recommendation_text: Homozygous individuals should discuss iron level monitoring (serum ferritin, transferrin saturation) with a clinician.

CC.effect_summary: No HFE H63D mutation. Normal iron regulation at this locus.

CG.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE H63D. Minimal clinical significance alone. May contribute to iron overload risk with compound C282Y/H63D genotype.

GC.effect_summary: Carrier of HFE H63D. Minimal clinical significance alone.

GG.effect_summary: Homozygous HFE H63D. Mildly increased iron absorption. Lower penetrance than C282Y.

snps.recommendation_text: H63D alone has low clinical penetrance. Most relevant in compound heterozygosity with C282Y.

pathways.name: Lactose Tolerance
pathways.description: Variants affecting lactase persistence and lactose digestion in adulthood.

AA.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype. Likely able to digest lactose in adulthood.

AG.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype (one functional allele). Likely able to digest lactose in adulthood.

GA.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype (one functional allele). Likely able to digest lactose in adulthood.

GG.effect_summary: Lactase non-persistent genotype. Likely reduced ability to digest lactose in adulthood.

snps.recommendation_text: Lactase non-persistent individuals may benefit from lactose-free dairy products or lactase enzyme supplements.

ancestry_caveat.caveat_text: Ancestry note: this non-persistence call relies on the European/South-Asian lactase marker (LCT -13910C>T, rs4988235). Lactase persistence in African and Middle Eastern populations is driven by other LCT enhancer variants: this panel now assays -13915*G (rs41380347, Arab/Middle-Eastern) and -13907*G (rs41525747, East African) — if a persistence allele is detected at either, you are likely lactase-persistent despite this result. The -14010*C (rs145946881) and -14009*G (rs869051967) alleles are not on the supported consumer arrays and are not assayed, so reduced lactase digestion cannot be confidently concluded for non-European/South-Asian ancestry (PMID 15106124, 19575818, 29063188, 17159977).

TT.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype at secondary regulatory locus.

TC.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype at secondary regulatory locus.

CT.effect_summary: Lactase persistent genotype at secondary regulatory locus.

CC.effect_summary: Supports lactase non-persistence. Confirms finding from rs4988235 if both present.

snps.recommendation_text: Supportive variant for lactose tolerance assessment. Most informative when combined with rs4988235.

ancestry_caveat.caveat_text: Ancestry note: LCT -22018 (rs182549) is a secondary marker in tight linkage with the European/South-Asian -13910 lactase haplotype (Enattah 2002), not an independent functional variant, so a non-persistence-supporting result here carries the same ancestry limitation as the rs4988235 -13910 call. African and Middle Eastern lactase persistence is driven by independent LCT enhancer variants (e.g. -14010*C, -13915*G, -13907*G) on different haplotype backgrounds that this panel does not assay, so reduced lactase digestion cannot be confidently concluded for non-European/South-Asian ancestry (PMID 11788828, 17159977, 29063188).

CC.effect_summary: Two copies of the LCT -13915*G lactase-persistence allele (rs41380347). Lactase persistent — likely able to digest lactose in adulthood. This Arab/Middle-Eastern persistence variant acts independently of the European -13910 marker.

AC.effect_summary: One copy of the LCT -13915*G lactase-persistence allele (rs41380347). Lactase persistent — likely able to digest lactose in adulthood (Arab/Middle-Eastern persistence variant, independent of the European -13910 marker).

CA.effect_summary: One copy of the LCT -13915*G lactase-persistence allele (rs41380347). Lactase persistent — likely able to digest lactose in adulthood (Arab/Middle-Eastern persistence variant, independent of the European -13910 marker).

AA.effect_summary: No LCT -13915*G persistence allele at this Arab/Middle-Eastern marker. This does not establish lactose non-persistence on its own — persistence can arise from the European -13910*T or other LCT enhancer variants (some not assayed here).

snps.recommendation_text: A lactase-persistence enhancer allele here positively indicates lactose tolerance and qualifies any non-persistence call from the European -13910 marker (rs4988235).

CG.effect_summary: One copy of the LCT -13907*G lactase-persistence allele (rs41525747). Lactase persistent — likely able to digest lactose in adulthood (East African persistence variant, independent of the European -13910 marker). The heterozygous C/G call is strand-invariant.

GC.effect_summary: One copy of the LCT -13907*G lactase-persistence allele (rs41525747). Lactase persistent — likely able to digest lactose in adulthood (East African persistence variant, independent of the European -13910 marker). The heterozygous C/G call is strand-invariant.

CC.effect_summary: Would be two copies of the -13907*G persistence allele (lactase persistent). Because rs41525747 is a palindromic (C/G) SNP, the array strand cannot be resolved, so this homozygous call is withheld as indeterminate at runtime rather than risking a strand-flipped read; confirm by sequencing. Only the strand-invariant heterozygous call is scored.

GG.effect_summary: Would be the ancestral homozygote (no -13907*G persistence allele). Because rs41525747 is a palindromic (C/G) SNP, the array strand cannot be resolved, so this homozygous call is withheld as indeterminate at runtime rather than risking a strand-flipped read; confirm by sequencing. Only the strand-invariant heterozygous call is scored.

snps.recommendation_text: A lactase-persistence enhancer allele here positively indicates lactose tolerance and qualifies any non-persistence call from the European -13910 marker (rs4988235).

TCF7L2.note: TCF7L2 rs7903146 is included in GWAS Catalog data for Type 2 diabetes risk factor. Cross-referenced from metabolic GWAS findings but not a primary nutrigenomics pathway variant.

PPARG.note: PPARG Pro12Ala (rs1801282) relates to insulin sensitivity and fat metabolism. Referenced via GWAS associations.

MC4R.note: MC4R variants affect appetite regulation and BMI. Referenced via GWAS associations for obesity-related traits.

FTO.note: FTO rs9939609 is a well-replicated obesity/BMI variant. Referenced via GWAS associations.

APOE.note: APOE lipid/dietary findings are generated by the dedicated APOE module (P3-22). Not scored in the nutrigenomics pathway cards to avoid duplication.
